name: lazygit (Terminal UI for Git)

auth.notes: Uses local git credentials; no external auth

performance.notes: TUI; native git operations; very fast

agent_readiness.notes: Terminal UI for git operations. Not an SDK — a standalone CLI tool. Single keystroke for stage/unstage, commit, push, pull, rebase, cherry-pick. Branch management. Interactive rebase. Conflict resolution. Customizable keybindings. Lazydocker companion. Very popular among terminal-first developers. Installed via brew, apt, winget. Custom user.yaml config. Integration with Neovim, tmux. Competing tools: tig (simpler), gitkraken (GUI), GitLens (VS Code).

gotchas: Some advanced git operations require fallback to CLI
best_when: Developers wanting fast terminal-based git workflow
Interactive staging, committing, rebasing without leaving terminal
Neovim/tmux users wanting git TUI integration
Reviewing diffs and managing branches interactively
avoid_when: GUI preference (use GitKraken, Fork, or VS Code)
summary: lazygit is a terminal UI for git providing single-keystroke staging, committing, rebasing, and branch management. Very popular among terminal-first developers. Single binary, no config required. Interactive rebase, conflict resolution, diff review. Not an SDK — a standalone CLI tool. Best for developers wanting a fast, keyboard-driven git workflow without leaving the terminal.
